prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a static product shot of a cereal box on a wooden surface. The box is prominently labeled “Kellogg’s APPLE RAISIN CRISP,” with illustrations of red apples on one side and purple grapes/raisins on the other. Real apples are arranged around the box, and cereal pieces are visible near the bottom front. A diagonal banner on the box reads “NEW! BETTER THAN EVER!” The lighting is warm and focused on the product, with darker edges around the frame.

By around 15 seconds, the image has changed to a close-up of a digital wristwatch. The watch has a black strap and a rectangular face with red/orange trim. The brand name “TIMEX” is visible on the left side of the face, along with small printed feature labels. The digital display shows large segmented numerals, though the exact time is not fully clear. The background is stylized and colorful, with a glowing red Christmas-tree-like shape and tiny decorative figures or silhouettes near the top of the watch. The overall look suggests a product display with a festive or decorative backdrop.

The final sampled frame is almost completely black, indicating a fade-out or transition to darkness. No visible product or text can be identified in this frame.
